global_context.scene_description: A full-frame digital UI mockup that fills the ENTIRE image — no phone, no hand, no real-world context. The frame IS the screen. Designed to look exactly like a Google Flights / Kayak airline booking calendar interface, but for AC installation pricing instead of flights. Header bar at the top in bold black sans-serif: 'AC INSTALL PRICING — PHOENIX'. Below the header, a sub-header row showing month tabs: MAY · JUNE · JULY · AUG, with a small flight-calendar-style icon. Then a calendar grid filling 80% of the frame, showing a 3-month view. Each date cell is a colored rectangle with a price tile inside, color-coded like an airline pricing calendar: early MAY dates are kelly-green '$8,500', mid-MAY '$9,000', late MAY into early JUNE yellow-green '$9,500-$10,500', mid-JUNE yellow-orange '$11,500-$12,500', late JUNE into early JULY orange-red '$13,000-$13,500', mid-late JULY DEEP RED '$14,500-$15,500' with 'PEAK' tags on the deepest red dates. The visual gradient goes green → yellow → orange → red across the calendar, telling the entire story at a glance. Today's date is subtly highlighted with a small darker outline. Bottom of frame has small fine-print sub-text in light grey: 'Prices rise as Phoenix summer demand spikes. Book off-season to lock in lowest rates.' The interface has clean subtle drop-shadow and modern airline-booking-app aesthetic. NO phone bezel, NO hand, NO real-world environment — pure UI mockup that fills the entire square frame edge-to-edge.

composition.framing: full-frame UI mockup, edge-to-edge, no real-world context
composition.focal_point: the color-graded calendar — green May fading through yellow June into red July

each cell is a price tile color-coded: GREEN for May ($8,500-$9,500), YELLOW-GREEN for late May ($9,500), YELLOW-ORANGE for June ($10,500-$12,500), ORANGE-RED for early July ($13,000-$13,500), DEEP RED for late July ($14,500-$15,500) with 'PEAK' label on the deepest dates
each cell shows date number small in the corner and price prominently in the center
color gradient flows visually across the calendar from green to red — the story is the gradient
